fit_reason: Strong fit: Kennedy Valve is a real U.S. manufacturer of waterworks, fire-protection, hydrant and valve products, with foundry, machining, assembly, engineering, quality, MRP/MPS planning, and recent supplier-cost and planning-pressure signals.
manufacturing_profile: Kennedy Valve, part of McWane, manufactures fire hydrants, iHydrant monitoring products, resilient/double/rotating/solid wedge gate valves, butterfly valves, check valves, eccentric plug valves, in-plant valves, accessories, and UL-FM fire products. Its catalog appears high-SKU/high-variant: the website lists hydrant and gate-valve result sets, and McWane says Kennedy manufactures a complete line from its Elmira, NY facility for waterworks, potable/wastewater treatment, and fire-protection markets. Public job posts describe the site as a gray and ductile iron foundry plus computerized machining and assembly operation; ISO scope covers design and manufacture of gray/ductile iron castings for hydrants, indicator posts, check, gate, butterfly valves, and components.

recent_triggers.trigger: Supplier-cost driven price increases on Kennedy Valve XBFV, resilient wedge gate valves, indicator/wall posts, and check valves
recent_triggers.why_it_matters: A distributor notice cited escalating supplier costs and 3-5% Kennedy price increases effective April 25, 2026, which is directly relevant to procurement automation, supplier tracking, and cost-change workflows.

recent_triggers.trigger: Hiring for Value Stream Material & Production Planner
recent_triggers.why_it_matters: The role owns MPS/MRP execution, material availability, shortage recovery, schedule adherence, and recovery after production disruptions; this is a clean signal that planning/procurement coordination is operationally important.
The scope covers design/manufacture of castings and valve/hydrant components, suggesting quality-controlled supplier and production records where procurement-agent audit trails could matter.

recent_triggers.trigger: U.S. Rep. Nick Langworthy visited Kennedy Valve after moving district office to Elmira
recent_triggers.why_it_matters: The company was publicly framed as one of the area's largest employers and an economic catalyst, reinforcing the scale and visibility of its Elmira manufacturing operation.

recommended_contact.name: Chris McCutcheon, CMRP
recommended_contact.title: Supply Chain Director
recommended_contact.why_this_person: Best primary buyer: public org data lists Chris as Supply Chain Director, and the current planning/supplier-cost triggers sit squarely in supply chain, MRP, material availability, and shortage recovery.

outreach_angle: Cronwell should lead with Kennedy's current material-planning pressure: a high-variant valve/hydrant catalog, foundry-machining-assembly operations, 2026 supplier-cost price movement, and active MRP/MPS planning hires. Position agents as a way to keep supplier price changes, shortage recovery, material readiness, and PO follow-up synchronized before they disrupt production schedules.
open_questions: Which ERP/MRP system Kennedy Valve uses and whether purchasing is centralized at Kennedy, McWane Valve & Hydrant, or McWane corporate.
How much direct spend is castings, machined components, coatings, rubber/elastomers, fasteners, sensors, packaging, and MRO.
Whether Chris McCutcheon owns sourcing decisions directly or influences alongside plant GM, finance, and value-stream leaders.

sources.title: Viking SupplyNet pricing announcement for Kennedy Valve and Talco
Kennedy Valve Value Stream Material & Production Planner posting

baseline_note: Approximate elapsed time reported by fresh Codex agent: 15 minutes. Limitations: LinkedIn profile/email were not independently accessed beyond the provided lead data and public contact-directory snippets; private ERP, spend, supplier count, and procurement org structure remain unverified.
